subject: Physics
title: How the Sun Makes Energy
description: A micro-lesson that makes How the Sun Makes Energy usable.

hook.text: The Sun fuses 600 million tons of hydrogen into helium every second. That's where sunlight comes from.

buildup.text: At its core, temperatures reach 15 million degrees. Hydrogen nuclei slam together with immense energy.

discovery.text: Four hydrogen nuclei fuse into one helium nucleus, releasing energy because helium weighs slightly less.

twist.text: The missing mass converts to energy via E = mc². Four million tons of mass become sunlight every second.

climax.text: The Sun has been fusing hydrogen for 4.6 billion years and has enough fuel for 5 billion more.

punchline.text: Hydrogen becomes helium. Lost mass becomes sunlight. Fusion powers stars.

quiz.question: What nuclear process powers the Sun?
